nodes.label: EEVideo Stream Compatibility Profile v1
nodes.rationale: The repository focuses on current public host-side compatibility behavior rather than a full native EEVideo transport stack.

nodes.label: EEVideo Stream Compatibility Profile v1
nodes.rationale: The profile is intentionally narrower than broader EEVideo specification work so independent implementations can match current host-side behavior without guessing.

nodes.label: Control-Plane Boundary
nodes.rationale: The v1 profile is transport-only; stream lifecycle intent is routed through an internal abstraction so future CoAP/register control can be added without rewriting packetization or socket I/O.

nodes.label: Public goeevideo Alignment
nodes.rationale: Where prose specifications and public code differ, this repository follows the public Go implementation.

nodes.label: Multicast Same-Port Fanout
nodes.rationale: Same-port fanout uses IPv4 multicast because two unicast listeners cannot both bind the same local UDP socket.

nodes.label: pipeline Provider
nodes.rationale: The pipeline provider keeps the full source pipeline operator-owned and requires an appsink named framesink with caps matching the configured mode.

nodes.label: argus Provider
nodes.rationale: The built-in argus provider remains available but is not the recommended Jetson path because this repository lacks matching hardware coverage.

nodes.label: Linux Provider Bring-Up Order
nodes.rationale: Start with synthetic to prove discovery/control/streaming without hardware, then move to v4l2 for low-friction real hardware, and use pipeline as an escape hatch.

nodes.label: Jetson Nano JetPack 4.x First-Time EEVideo Bring-Up
nodes.norm_label: jetson nano jetpack 4.x first-time eevideo bring-up

nodes.label: Jetson Native Pipeline Bring-Up
nodes.rationale: For Jetson bring-up, build directly on the Jetson and run an explicit nvarguscamerasrc-to-appsink pipeline; cross-build helpers are fallback assets.

nodes.label: Jetson Cross-Build Fallback
nodes.rationale: Cross-building is kept as an experimental or convenience fallback when a local Jetson build is not practical.
